vendors.name: Gestion BTP
vendors.tagline: Logiciel BTP web + comptabilité, tout-en-un pour les entreprises marocaines
vendors.market_position: A Moroccan-native, web-based all-in-one for construction firms — chantiers, devis, facturation, comptabilité analytique, liasse fiscale and CNSS in one product. It targets the exact gap where imported tools like EBP and Sage Batigest cover quotes and invoices but not Moroccan accounting and payroll.
pros: Covers accounting/payroll where imports do not
Web + a "chef de chantier" mobile app
cons: Depth vs Sage on heavy BTP workflows unproven
vendors.ma_specific_note: Built around Moroccan compliance — TVA multi-taux (20/14/10/7/0%), liasse fiscale DGI, CNSS declarations and MAD — so a contractor gets devis-to-accounting in one local system rather than bolting an import onto a separate comptabilité tool.

vendors.name: EBP Bâtiment (Édition Maroc)
vendors.tagline: Devis, facturation et gestion de chantier pour le bâtiment, adapté au Maroc
vendors.market_position: French-origin building software with a dedicated Édition Maroc — strong on quotes, factures d’avancement / situations de travaux, retenue de garantie and work libraries (Batiprix/BatiChiffrage). The default "serious artisan/SME" devis-facture tool in Morocco.
cons: No Moroccan comptabilité/paie — needs a separate tool
Core support is France-based; some UI still shows EUR
vendors.typical_pricing: Roughly 8,000–15,000 MAD/year for the devis/facture scope; reseller-quoted.
vendors.ma_specific_note: The localized Moroccan edition is sold through certified resellers (e.g. Ciel Informatique) but does not include Moroccan accounting or payroll — Moroccan firms pair it with a separate comptabilité tool to close the loop.

vendors.name: Sage Batigest Connect
vendors.tagline: Solution de gestion de chantier pour les entrepreneurs et artisans du bâtiment
vendors.market_position: The heavyweight BTP option for mid/large Moroccan contractors — a specialised quote→situation workflow that links to Sage 100 for accounting, deployed through an established Moroccan reseller network. Chosen by firms that want the industry-standard name.
vendors.typical_pricing: Roughly 15,000–80,000 MAD/year by version (plus Sage 100); reseller-quoted.
vendors.ma_specific_note: Moroccan compliance (DGI filings, CNSS) is handled via the paired Sage 100 module rather than natively in Batigest, and pricing is reseller-dependent in MAD — so budget for the full Sage stack, not just Batigest.

vendors.name: Odoo (BTP, via intégrateurs marocains)
vendors.tagline: ERP intégré pour le BTP marocain — chantier, compta, paie et stock en un seul système
vendors.market_position: The global open-source ERP, localized by Moroccan integrators into a full BTP stack. The go-to when a contractor wants ONE system (projects + invoicing + stock + payroll + accounting) instead of EBP/Sage plus a separate accounting tool.
pros: Full integrated ERP — no double entry
Native Moroccan accounting/payroll via the integrator
Eligible for the MOWAKABA digitization subsidy
cons: BTP modules are not native — it is an integration project
vendors.typical_pricing: Community is free; Enterprise ~280 MAD/user/mo; integration roughly 40,000–300,000 MAD in year one.
vendors.ma_specific_note: Moroccan integrators add native PCGE accounting, TVA marocaine, CNSS/AMO/IR payroll and labour-code overtime (25%/50%) with FR/AR support — and the MOWAKABA program can subsidise up to 90% (TPE) / 80% (PME) of the digitization cost.

vendors.name: Hisab
vendors.tagline: Plateforme moderne de facturation électronique du Maroc — conforme DGI
vendors.market_position: A modern cloud e-invoicing platform for Moroccan SMEs and accountants. Not construction-specific, but highly relevant as the DGI e-invoicing mandate lands — any trades business needing compliant devis and factures can use it. Clean, bilingual, compliance-first.
vendors.ma_specific_note: Generates structured UBL 2.1 XML with sequential numbering, auto ICE/IF/TVA fields, a digital signature/integrity chain and 10-year archiving — i.e. the technical shape the DGI clearance mandate will require, ahead of the official API.

vendors.name: Fawatir AI
vendors.tagline: Logiciel de gestion conforme DGI au Maroc — facturation, paie, POS, stocks
vendors.market_position: A Moroccan AI-assisted invoicing and management suite, positioned for auto-entrepreneur / self-employed status — relevant to solo artisans and very small trades businesses that need DGI-compliant invoices quickly.
vendors.ma_specific_note: DGI-compliant invoicing with auto-population of the mandatory fields (ICE/IF/TVA), aimed at the auto-entrepreneur regime most solo Moroccan tradespeople operate under — useful for fast compliant quoting rather than site management.

vendors.name: Aube
vendors.tagline: Devis et facturation conformes au Maroc — TVA correcte, vérification ICE client
vendors.market_position: A Moroccan devis/facturation tool that leads on legal conformity — correct TVA, client ICE verification, compliant quotes. A horizontal SME tool a trades business can use for compliant quoting and invoicing.
vendors.ma_specific_note: Automatic compliance with Moroccan invoicing rules — correct multi-rate TVA and customer ICE verification — which is the unglamorous detail that decides whether a devis is legally valid as the e-invoicing regime tightens.

vendors.name: Ciel Informatique
vendors.tagline: Intégrateur marocain Sage & EBP — gestion de chantier, devis, conformité DGI
vendors.market_position: A Casablanca-based reseller/integrator of Sage (50/100, Batigest Connect) and EBP — the local implementation and support channel for the imported BTP tools, covering training, DGI/CNSS setup and consulting. Listed because in Morocco you generally buy Sage/EBP through a partner like this.
cons: A reseller/integrator, not a product itself
vendors.ma_specific_note: This is how most Moroccan firms actually acquire Sage or EBP — the integrator localizes the install (DGI filings, CNSS declarations) so the imported tool fits Moroccan compliance out of the gate.

vendors.name: Oasis Techno Cloud
vendors.tagline: Intégrateur Odoo spécialisé BTP au Maroc — ERP chantier, compta PCGE, paie marocaine
vendors.market_position: A Moroccan Odoo integrator specialised in BTP — the partner that turns generic Odoo into a Morocco-compliant construction ERP. Listed as the implementation route for the Odoo option (and it publishes detailed Moroccan BTP-software comparisons).
vendors.ma_specific_note: The practical way to get Odoo working as a Moroccan BTP ERP — native PCGE accounting, TVA marocaine and CNSS/AMO/IR payroll built in, with guidance on the MOWAKABA digitization subsidy.

vendors.name: Datao
vendors.tagline: Repérez les meilleurs appels d’offres BTP au Maroc grâce à l’IA
vendors.market_position: A Moroccan public-tender intelligence platform (UXS SARL AU, Rabat) that aggregates construction marchés publics with AI analysis of the cahier des charges and qualification matching. The strongest genuine construction project-intelligence play in Morocco.
pros: Real BTP tender data at scale (12,000+ from 300+ sources)
cons: Public tenders only — not private renovation leads
vendors.typical_pricing: Freemium — free account; paid tiers not disclosed.
vendors.ma_specific_note: Pulls 12,000+ construction tenders from 300+ official Moroccan sources (regional agencies, hydraulic basins, municipalities) and matches each against your Ministry qualification class — the Moroccan version of "which public jobs can I actually bid".

vendors.name: Aljady
vendors.tagline: Le guide de référence des marchés publics au Maroc — veille et alertes IA
vendors.market_position: A Moroccan public-procurement intelligence platform — broad (90,000+ tenders across all sectors including BTP) with AI alerts, a reference-price tool and competitor/bid-history analytics. Complements Datao; more horizontal but covers construction strongly.
vendors.typical_pricing: Subscription with a 7-day free trial; tiers not disclosed.
vendors.ma_specific_note: Real-time veille on Moroccan marchés publics that extracts the bordereaux de prix, lots and quantities and surfaces competitors’ submission history and success rates — useful for pricing a public bid in a thin-margin market.

vendors.name: Sabab.ma
vendors.tagline: Trouvez un plombier, électricien ou artisan de confiance au Maroc
vendors.market_position: A leading Moroccan artisan/home-services marketplace connecting individuals to verified, geolocated tradespeople (plumbing, electrical, painting, HVAC, appliance repair) — the closest Morocco equivalent to a renovation/artisan lead marketplace.
pros: Nationwide (Casablanca, Rabat, Marrakech, Tanger, Fès, Agadir)
cons: No-commission model — lead-gen value to pros is unclear
vendors.typical_pricing: Free for clients; no commission (payment direct client↔pro).
vendors.ma_specific_note: Free, no-commission and ID-verified — good for a Moroccan artisan to be found, but because it takes no cut there is no paid-lead funnel, so treat it as a visibility channel rather than a lead-purchase one.

vendors.name: Bricole
vendors.tagline: Votre artisan, à portée de main
vendors.market_position: An app-first Moroccan home-services marketplace at real scale (25k+ registered pros, 100k+ downloads, 4.6★). 160+ services skewed toward trades — plumbing, electrical, painting, HVAC, carpentry, tiling/marble. Operates in Morocco and Egypt.
cons: Zero-commission — weak monetization story for pros
vendors.ma_specific_note: The largest of the consumer artisan apps by traction, but fully free/zero-commission, so it is a discovery channel for a Moroccan tradesperson rather than a paid lead source.

vendors.name: Allopro (ex-Bricall)
vendors.tagline: Plateforme marocaine de services à domicile — devis multiples, pros vérifiés
vendors.market_position: A Moroccan home-services marketplace with an actual lead-gen / paid-listing model — pros pay a subscription to receive leads, and clients get up to five devis from verified providers. The most WrenchStack-aligned monetization model among the consumer marketplaces.
vendors.typical_pricing: Pro subscription (tiers not public); free for clients.
vendors.ma_specific_note: Unlike the free marketplaces, Allopro actually charges pros for leads and sends clients up to five competing devis — so it is the closest thing to a Western "pay-for-leads" model in Morocco, albeit across mixed home services.

vendors.name: Bricoul.ma
vendors.tagline: Connecter particuliers et artisans vérifiés (CIN, RC, certifications)
vendors.market_position: A Moroccan artisan marketplace with explicit paid "forfaits" (subscription packages) for artisans and rigorous verification (CIN, RC, certifications). Trades-focused — plumber, electrician, painter, carpenter, HVAC, glazier. Another genuine paid-lead-gen entry.
vendors.ma_specific_note: Verifies artisans against CIN, RC and certifications and sells them lead packages — a more trust-vetted, genuinely paid lead channel, though currently smaller and Casablanca-weighted.

vendors.name: Wafa Assurance
vendors.tagline: Le leader marocain — Tous Risques Chantier et décennale pour le BTP
vendors.market_position: Wafa Assurance (Attijariwafa bank group) is the market-leading Moroccan insurer, offering a dedicated Tous Risques Chantier product plus complementary RC Décennale and targeting public and private BTP players of every size, with nationwide bancassurance distribution.
pros: Market leader with deep BTP underwriting capacity
Both mandatory covers (TRC + RCD) under one roof
cons: No online quote — agent/broker underwriting only
RCD gated on a contrôle technique
vendors.typical_pricing: Project-rated (premium as a % of total works value); quoted per chantier.
vendors.ma_specific_note: TRC covers damage to the works plus the civil liability of every site stakeholder, while RCD covers collapse risk for ten years post-reception under Morocco’s post-2024 mandate — but RCD subscription requires a prior contrôle technique by an approved body.

vendors.name: RMA (Royale Marocaine d’Assurance)
vendors.tagline: Grand assureur marocain avec une gamme BTP dédiée
vendors.market_position: RMA (FinanceCom group; founded 1949) is among Morocco’s top-tier insurers and runs a dedicated BTP offering covering Tous Risques Chantier and RC Décennale with project-tailored solutions.
pros: Dedicated BTP product page and underwriting team
Large balance sheet for big infrastructure risks
vendors.ma_specific_note: Frames its décennale cover around Article 769 of the DOC — indemnifying structural damage that compromises solidity within the ten-year period — i.e. built for the Moroccan legal framework rather than a ported European wording.

vendors.name: AtlantaSanad Assurance
vendors.tagline: Produits BTP nommés — Tous Risques Chantiers + décennale « ALBINAA »
vendors.market_position: AtlantaSanad (from the Atlanta–Sanad merger) is a major Moroccan insurer with explicitly branded construction products: a Tous Risques Chantiers policy and a dedicated décennale product marketed as "RC Décennale ALBINAA".
cons: The ALBINAA deep-link can be hard to find — ask the agent for it
vendors.typical_pricing: Project-rated; quote on request via agent.
vendors.ma_specific_note: One of the few insurers to give the décennale cover its own product name (ALBINAA — Arabic for "building"), which makes it easy to ask an agent for exactly the mandatory cover under the post-2024 regime.

vendors.name: AXA Assurance Maroc
vendors.tagline: « BATISSUR » — TRC et montage, avec décennale intégrée
vendors.market_position: AXA Assurance Maroc, the local arm of the global AXA group, markets a named construction product "BATISSUR" (Tous Risques Chantier / Montage) plus "MIHNASSUR" professional multirisque — international brand credibility in a fully localized Moroccan entity.
cons: Décennale still gated on contrôle technique
vendors.ma_specific_note: BATISSUR covers every stakeholder on one chantier (architects, engineers, workers, suppliers, BET) through the works and the maintenance period, with the décennale element covering structural integrity for ten years — a strong fit for a contractor who wants a global brand locally.

vendors.name: Allianz Maroc
vendors.tagline: Page sectorielle BTP dédiée — chantier + décennale
vendors.market_position: Allianz Maroc (global Allianz group, locally licensed) runs a dedicated Bâtiment et Travaux Publics sector page and a risques-techniques line covering construction projects from site installation through completion, including décennale liability for ten years post-completion.
cons: Cover described more generically than rivals’ named products
vendors.ma_specific_note: Positions décennale as a legal obligation for all building-works providers and covers both the construction phase and the ten-year post-reception liability — international underwriting standards delivered through local agents.

vendors.name: Sage Paie & RH Maroc (Sage 100)
vendors.tagline: Le standard de paie localisé des PME marocaines
vendors.market_position: Sage is the long-established payroll + accounting standard among Moroccan SMEs and accountants. Sage 100 Paie & RH integrates current Moroccan tax/social rules — IR, CNSS, AMO, CIMR, IS — and auto-computes statutory contributions, distributed through a wide local reseller network.
pros: Deep local installed base and accountant familiarity
cons: Licence + integrator cost (not cheap for micro-firms)
vendors.ma_specific_note: Handles Moroccan IR-at-source, CNSS (with the ~9,000 MAD ceiling), AMO and CIMR, and is usually paired with Damancom EDI workflows — the safe default if your accountant already lives in Sage.

vendors.name: OJRA — Paie Maroc (NSE)
vendors.tagline: Paie marocaine avec EDI Damancom/CNSS natif
vendors.market_position: OJRA, developed by Moroccan vendor NSE, is a local-market payroll product purpose-built for Moroccan rules, with a native EDI module for Damancom (CNSS declarations) and full IR/AMO handling — a fit for firms wanting a homegrown, locally supported tool.
pros: Lighter and more affordable than a full ERP
cons: Narrower feature depth than a full HRIS
vendors.ma_specific_note: CNSS Damancom EDI is built in rather than bolted on, so monthly salary declarations transmit natively — a practical edge for a Moroccan SME that just needs payroll to be compliant and quiet.

vendors.name: Humantal
vendors.tagline: Paie & RH SaaS spécialisée BTP au Maroc — multi-chantiers, pointage GPS
vendors.market_position: A Moroccan payroll/HR SaaS built specifically for BTP — filling the exact gap EBP and Sage Batigest leave open. It targets contractors managing crews across multiple sites with site-based time tracking and construction-specific pay rules.
pros: Pairs well with EBP/Sage devis tools
cons: Payroll/HR only — not a full ERP
vendors.typical_pricing: Free quote (subscription; no public flat price).
vendors.ma_specific_note: Handles the messy parts of Moroccan construction payroll specifically — GPS site pointage, multi-chantier crews, the BTP convention collective and intérimaires — and files CNSS via Damancom in one click. Pair it with EBP or Sage Batigest for the devis side.

vendors.name: Qualification & Classification des Entreprises de BTP
vendors.tagline: Le passeport des marchés publics pour les entreprises de BTP marocaines
vendors.market_position: A state system (Décret n°2-94-223 of 16 June 1994) run by the Ministère de l’Équipement et de l’Eau with the FNBTP. A qualification + classification certificate is mandatory to bid on public construction tenders above set thresholds.
pros: Unlocks eligibility for public BTP markets
Structured by sector + class (19 sectors / 115 qualifications)
cons: Mandatory only above certain thresholds — small private jobs don’t need it
Your class limits the project size you can bid
Tied to demonstrated past works + means
vendors.ma_specific_note: Covers 19 activity sectors and 115 qualifications across classes 1–5 plus a superior "S" class — without it, a Moroccan firm cannot access ministry, municipality or public-institution tenders, which is most of the World-Cup-2030 pipeline.

vendors.name: CNSS — Immatriculation employeur + Damancom
vendors.tagline: L’immatriculation sécurité sociale obligatoire de tout employeur marocain
vendors.market_position: Every Moroccan employer must register with the CNSS and declare/pay social contributions; Damancom is the CNSS online télédéclaration/EDI portal. It covers AMO (mandatory health), family/social benefits and pensions.
pros: A CNSS number feeds the ICE
vendors.typical_pricing: Statutory contributions (employer + employee % of salary, capped).
vendors.ma_specific_note: A CNSS affiliation number is one of the legal identifiers feeding the ICE, and salary declarations run through Damancom (EDI) — which is exactly why Moroccan payroll tools (OJRA, Humantal, Sage) integrate there natively.

vendors.name: ICE — Identifiant Commun de l’Entreprise
vendors.tagline: L’identifiant unique à 15 chiffres, obligatoire sur chaque facture
vendors.market_position: The ICE uniquely identifies a Moroccan company and its establishments across all administrations, and has been mandatory since January 2016 on all official documents — invoices, devis, tax declarations and accounting records.
pros: Mandatory on invoices (central to the e-invoicing regime)
cons: Must appear on all commercial/fiscal documents — omission = a non-compliant invoice
Obtained through different bodies by legal form
Don’t confuse it with the IF (tax ID) or RC number
vendors.ma_specific_note: 15 digits (9 entity + 4 establishment + 2 control key), attributed by OMPIC for new companies or the DGI for individuals — and it will sit on every e-invoice, so any invoicing tool you choose must carry and validate it.

vendors.name: Registre de Commerce (RC) — OMPIC
vendors.tagline: L’immatriculation commerciale obligatoire pour exister et contracter
vendors.market_position: Registration in the Registre de Commerce (local commercial court, centralized at OMPIC) is the foundational legal step to incorporate, starting with the certificat négatif (name reservation) at OMPIC.
pros: Name protection via the certificat négatif
cons: Required up front (certificat négatif → RC → IF → CNSS → ICE)
Statutory updates needed on changes (address, capital, managers)
vendors.ma_specific_note: The RC number is one of the core legal identifiers (with the IF and CNSS) consolidated under the ICE — the first domino in the sequence a Moroccan contractor completes before they can legally invoice or bid.

vendors.name: DGI — Identifiant Fiscal (IF) & portail SIMPL
vendors.tagline: Immatriculation fiscale + le portail DGI pour toutes les déclarations
vendors.market_position: The Direction Générale des Impôts issues the Identifiant Fiscal (IF) and operates SIMPL for online declaration and payment of TVA, IS and IR. Télédéclaration is now the norm, and the upcoming e-invoicing platform sits alongside SIMPL.
cons: Rates/brackets shift each Loi de Finances
vendors.ma_specific_note: This is the same authority running the phased 2026 e-invoicing clearance mandate — your IF feeds the ICE, SIMPL handles TVA/IS/IR today, and the new e-invoicing platform is what your invoicing software will have to connect to next.
